goal: Determine whether TAC's validated persistent-computation mechanisms survive real language-model scaling from 30M-50M to about 112M parameters.

current_assessment.commercial_story: context compression remains the strongest current commercial angle

continue_if: TAC training is stable at matched token and compute budget.
Routing entropy and program utilization do not collapse.
Memory utilization and state carry metrics remain positive.
Persistent-state, repair, or compression holdout advantages survive against the matched transformer.
stop_if: TAC loses the matched LM comparison and all mechanism holdouts.
Routing collapse, state dilution, or optimization interference explains the result.

tickets.title: Freeze TAC v0.1 branch structure
acceptance: main exists.
release/tac-v0.1 exists as the frozen v0.1 reference.
develop/tac-v0.2 exists for new scaling work.

tickets.title: Build 112M TAC and matched transformer configs
acceptance: configs/tac_v02_112m.py locks vocab_size=8192, d_model=512, n_layers=8, n_heads=8.
transformer_112m.py provides the matched transformer baseline.
Both configs are within 1 percent parameter gap.

tickets.title: Add metrics_v02 logging contract
acceptance: metrics_v02.json contains train loss, eval loss, perplexity, routing entropy, program utilization, memory utilization, and state carry metrics.
The schema normalizes raw trainer metrics for TAC and transformer records.

tickets.title: Prepare dataset builder and holdout split
acceptance: Language, code, long-horizon, and holdout streams are specified.
Validation holdout rows are written separately and documented as never-train data.
Offline smoke mode verifies split behavior without remote downloads.

tickets.title: Create public release and outreach artifacts
acceptance: Release audit exists.
Public progress page exists.
Researcher/founder outreach workbook exists.
Demo video source and rendered local MP4 exist.

tickets.title: Integrate TAC-235 native bottleneck into LM path
acceptance: The default TAC v0.2 LM path forces generation through state-conditioned native program computation.
The path has a causal knockout test at the language-model interface.
The old residual/bypass path remains available only as an ablation.

tickets.title: Kaggle mechanism reproduction
acceptance: Run TAC-235 on Kaggle/GPU.
Run TAC-242 on Kaggle/GPU.
Run TAC-272 on Kaggle/GPU.
Archive artifacts under runs/ or Kaggle output and compare to CPU results.

tickets.title: Run 30M-50M real language-model pilot
acceptance: Train transformer_50m first on real v0.2 data.
Train tac_50m second on the same data, tokens, and compute budget.
Measure perplexity, throughput, memory usage, and compression retention.

tickets.title: Run full 112M scaling experiment
acceptance: Do not launch bigger scaling solely from the LM50A result.
The attempted lm112a Kaggle kernel was canceled and is not counted as a 112M result.
Review TAC-280 checkpoint mechanism retests before spending the 112M run.
TAC-281 mechanism sharpening completed and no variant passed the scale gate.
Train transformer_112m first on real v0.2 data.
Train tac_112m second on the same data, tokens, and compute budget.
Run persistent-state, repair, and compression retests.
Fill docs/tac_v02_stage_gate.md comparison table with measured results.

tickets.title: Run TAC-280 checkpoint mechanism retest suite
acceptance: Run transformer_50m and tac_50m checkpoints from LM50A through the same post-training probes.
Measure persistent-state carry, repair trace reuse, compression/structure reuse, noisy-key retrieval, reset versus carry, and native bottleneck knockout.
Record whether TAC has a mechanism advantage after losing the plain LM comparison.
Document the result without claiming better LM, coding, math, planning, reasoning, or agent performance.

tickets.title: Draft scaling-retention paper
acceptance: Frame the paper around whether persistent computational states survive scale.
Report negative results if advantages disappear.
Avoid claiming competitive LM, coding, math, planning, or reasoning gains unless measured.
Draft written to docs/tac_v02_scaling_retention_report.md with the TAC-281 do-not-scale decision.

tickets.title: Run TAC-281 mechanism sharpening before scale
acceptance: Define late-bottleneck, small-adapter, and auxiliary-mechanism TAC variants in the 30M-50M parameter band.
Train each variant against the matched transformer baseline before any 112M run.
Retest each trained variant with the checkpoint mechanism suite.
Scale only if mechanism wins stay at least 3/4, carry stays positive, knockout delta stays positive, LM gap shrinks by at least 30%, and speed penalty drops.

Small adapter completed on Kaggle with 95.01% LM-gap shrink, 4.35x speed penalty, 3/4 mechanism wins, and positive carry, but failed the scale gate because bottleneck knockout delta was only 0.000021.
The first late bottleneck Kaggle run failed from an inactive identity-context shape bug and is not counted as a scientific result.
A fixed late bottleneck rerun completed on Kaggle as tac-v02-tac281-late-bottleneck-tac281b-latefix and failed the gate because the LM gap worsened despite mechanism retention.
Auxiliary mechanism completed on Kaggle as tac-v02-tac281-auxiliary-mechanism-tac281a and failed the gate because the LM gap did not shrink enough despite mechanism retention.
Combined TAC-281 decision is do_not_scale_yet with zero scale-ready variants.
